attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Edits Log — APPEND ONLY

This file is the permanent, immutable history of every change made in this project. Read it whenever you need full context on how the project got to its current state.

## Rules

1. **Never delete, overwrite, edit, or reformat existing entries.** Past entries are immutable. If you change your mind or revert something, add a NEW entry that references the old one — don't touch the original.
2. **Append-only.** New entries go at the bottom.
3. **Log every meaningful action**, including: file create/modify/delete, commands that change state, architectural decisions, dependencies added, bugs found, bugs fixed, refactors, dead-ends you backed out of. When in doubt, log it.
4. **Do not log:** trivial reads, navigation, file inspection, or your own thinking. Only log changes and decisions.

## Entry format (use exactly this)

---
## [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM TZ] — <short title>
**What:** <1–2 sentences on what was done>
**Why:** <the user request or reason that triggered it>
**Files touched:** <path — created | modified | deleted>
**Commands run:** <shell commands, if any>
**Outcome:** <result, including errors, partial success, or surprises>
**Notes:** <gotchas, half-finished work, things future-you should know>
---

## On reverts and mistakes

If you undo something, write a new entry titled "Revert: <original title>" and reference the original timestamp. The original entry stays. The log tells the truth about what happened, including wrong turns.

## On compaction

If this file gets long, do not summarize or prune it yourself. Ask the user before doing anything destructive.

---
name: project-bot-v2
description: "Polymarket Copy Bot v2.3 — full rebuild complete 2026-05-23. All 6 phases done. Run `uv run polymarket-bot start`, dashboard at localhost:8000."
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: a17cd5ee-147d-424f-af71-5a78117753ab
---

**SUPERSEDES the old v2 (python main.py / localhost:8080)**. That was a different codebase. The v2.3 rebuild is a complete from-scratch build in the same directory.

**Why:** Full rebuild to spec in `All about.md` (v2.3). PostgreSQL, uv package manager, asyncio, FastAPI dashboard.

**How to apply:** Next session should focus on provisioning PostgreSQL and first startup. Do NOT assume the old `python main.py` approach still works — it was replaced by the new CLI.

## Status: ALL 6 PHASES COMPLETE (2026-05-23)
- Phase 1: Core models/enums/exceptions/clock, config (settings, secrets, validators), DB (ORM, repos, migrations), Polymarket API client
- Phase 2: 13 metric files + composite_score.py, funnel stages A–F + orchestrator
- Phase 3: signal_normalizer, filters, sizer, paper_fill, order_engine, dedup, queue, poller
- Phase 4: positions/tracker, pricer, exiter, reconciler; risk/circuit_breakers, caps, killswitch; runner.py
- Phase 5: notifications/discord+ntfy+alerts; src/cli/main.py; FastAPI app + 6 routes + WebSocket; web/index.html dashboard
- Phase 6: 6 unit tests, 2 integration tests, fixtures, docker-compose.yml, Dockerfile, CI workflow, scripts/

## Next action to get running
1. Set `DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://...` in `.env` (Neon.tech free tier recommended)
2. `uv sync`
3. `uv run alembic upgrade head`
4. `uv run python scripts/seed_db.py`
5. `uv run polymarket-bot start` → dashboard at http://localhost:8000

## Key API facts (easy to get wrong)
- `get_trade_params(tier: int)` — takes tier int. Call `compute_tier(bankroll)` first.
- `EquitySnapshot` columns: `timestamp`, `cash_balance`, `position_value`, `total_equity`
- `WalletPoller.run(get_active_callable, get_shadow_callable)` — async callables, not lists
- `SignalEvent.side` = plain string `"YES"` or `"NO"`, not enum
- `SignalRepo.get_all_recent(limit)` for dashboard; `get_recent(wallet_id, limit)` for per-wallet

file.content:
---
name: project-strategy-overhaul
description: May 13 2026 — Strategy overhauled to match STRATEGY.md. Key filter changes and blockchain monitor disabled.
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 99da0246-6a02-4ad0-938f-767c310dfdae
---

Bot strategy overhauled on 2026-05-13 to match STRATEGY.md recommendations. STRATEGY.md is the source-of-truth research document for all strategy decisions.

**Why:** Previous config (100 min lead, 0.10–0.90 price band, 2% slippage, 20% stop-loss) was blocking 99.5% of signals. Working copy operators (Polycopy, Polycop, Polycule) use completely different defaults.

**Changes made:**
- `MIN_LEAD_TRADE_USD`: 100 → 5 (icebergers like Domer accumulate in small chunks)
- `MAX_PRICE_THRESHOLD`: 0.90 → 0.95 (was blocking bond strategies like Sharky6999)
- `MIN_PRICE_THRESHOLD`: 0.10 → 0.05 (was blocking underdog plays like RN1, Swiss Tony)
- `MAX_SLIPPAGE_PCT`: 2.0 → 10.0 (working operators use 5–10% market order cap)
- `STOP_LOSS_PERCENT`: 0.20 → 0.0 (disabled — primary exit is whale_exit mirror)
- `MAX_HOURS_TO_RESOLVE`: new — 1440h (60 days) upper bound added to risk_manager.py
- Blockchain monitor: disabled (was spamming 400 errors; Data API covers it)

**How to apply:** Always check STRATEGY.md before changing filter thresholds. The mirror-exit model (whale sells → we close) is the default exit and is already implemented in `_handle_whale_exit()` in `trade_executor.py`.

1	# Polymarket Copy-Trading Bot — Comprehensive Technical Planning Document
2	
3	**Version:** 1.0 · **Date:** May 24, 2026 · **Status:** Pre-build planning, paper-first
4	
5	---
6	
7	## TL;DR
8	
9	- **Build a Python (`py-clob-client-v2`) + SQLite + FastAPI + Next.js/shadcn/Lightweight-Charts local stack** that copies 20–50 daily-re-ranked Polymarket wallets via a hybrid Data-API-polling + Alchemy-Polygon-log-subscription detection layer, executes through FOK orders sized at $5 base (rank-multiplied $3.50–$7.50, floor $5) to clear the typical per-market `min_order_size` of 5 shares, and uses a single execution function whose only branch on `Mode.PAPER` vs `Mode.LIVE` is whether fills are written by a pessimistic in-memory simulator or by the live CLOB V2 client.
10	- **Treat paper as live.** All upstream logic — leader ingest, ranking, sizing, aggregation, conflict resolution, exits, capacity caps, kill-switch — is mode-agnostic; live mode is locked in the dashboard until paper validation criteria (14 consecutive days, ≥ 400 trades, > +3% net of simulated slippage, ≤ 8% max daily drawdown, zero ghost trades, zero unrecovered crashes) all hold within the last 14 days.
11	- **Free APIs are sufficient for everything.** Polymarket's CLOB, Gamma, and Data APIs are public (rates measured in thousands/10s); five Goldsky-hosted subgraphs and Alchemy's free Polygon tier (30M CU/month) cover discovery, ranking and chain-level audit; Dune Analytics' free tier (2,500 credits/month) provides cross-validation; no paid tier of any service is required for a $500-bankroll local bot.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## Key Findings
16	
17	1. **Polymarket migrated to CLOB V2 on April 28, 2026** — new exchange contracts, new collateral token (pUSD), and a fully deprecated V1 SDK; the bot MUST use `py-clob-client-v2` and the V2 endpoints.
18	2. **The CLOB user-channel websocket cannot subscribe to other users' wallets** — confirmed in Polymarket's documentation. Real-time copy-trading therefore needs a hybrid of (a) Data-API `/trades` polling per leader and (b) Polygon log subscription via Alchemy on the V2 Exchange contract, with Goldsky subgraphs as a reconciliation layer.
19	3. **The CLOB minimum order size is a per-market field** typically `"5"` (5 shares for limit orders); market orders are constrained at $1 USDC minimum notional. This is the single hardest sizing constraint on a $500 bankroll and is why fixed-dollar sizing (≥ $5/trade) beats proportional-to-leader sizing.
20	4. **Wash trading is structurally large on Polymarket** — a peer-reviewable study by Columbia and Barnard researchers (Kanoria, Ma, Sethi, Sirolly; SSRN, Nov 2025) found artificial trading averaged 25% of all buying and selling on Polymarket over the prior three years. The discovery layer must therefore include explicit anti-wash/cluster filters; uncritical leaderboard scraping is not safe.
21	5. **The Polymarket international platform reopened to US users in February 2026** (with state-level carve-outs); the bot does not need to bypass geofencing, but the user must confirm their jurisdiction's status before going live.
22	6. **The dashboard stack is decided:** Next.js 15 App Router + shadcn/ui + TradingView Lightweight Charts v5 (Apache-2.0, current version 5.2.0). This is the modern dark-trader-terminal pattern (see the `coinpulse` reference build using Next.js 16 + shadcn + Lightweight Charts) and avoids the licensing complexity of TradingView's proprietary Charting Library.
23	7. **OS-keychain-encrypted secrets, not plain `.env`.** The bot's EOA controls a Safe proxy holding real USDC; loss-of-key is total-loss. The default install reads private key + L2 credentials from the OS keychain via Python's `keyring`; the `.env` carries only references.

27	## Details
28	
29	### 1. Polymarket platform — state of play as of May 2026
30	
31	#### 1.1 Architecture
32	
33	Polymarket is a hybrid-decentralized CLOB on **Polygon PoS (chain ID 137)**. Off-chain matching, on-chain settlement via EIP-712 signed orders against the Exchange smart contract. There is no AMM in the user-facing trading flow today; the legacy FPMM contracts persist for on-chain conversions, splits, and merges, but routine trading is CLOB-only.
34	
35	**April 28, 2026 CLOB V2 cutover (the dominant fact for this build):**
36	
37	* Collateral migrated from **USDC.e (bridged) to pUSD (Polymarket USD)** — a Polygon ERC-20 backed 1:1 by Circle USDC, with backing enforced on-chain by the Collateral Onramp contract. The user-visible balance is still labeled USDC; pUSD is wire-level only.
38	* The Exchange contract was replaced with **CTF Exchange V2** (audited by Cantina and Quantstamp).
39	* The EIP-712 domain version bumped to `"2"` and the `verifyingContract` is the V2 Exchange.
40	* **The v1 py-clob-client and v1 TypeScript clob-client no longer work against production.** The v1 PyPI README explicitly directs: *"Please migrate to the V2 client: https://github.com/Polymarket/py-clob-client-v2."*
41	* All V1 resting orders were wiped during the ~1-hour maintenance window.
42	* Fees are now charged in pUSD at match time, not in shares at placement.
43	
44	**Other 2025–2026 changes that matter for a bot:**
45	
46	* In late April 2026, Polymarket's VP of Engineering DeFi publicly confirmed "chain migration" off Polygon as a roadmap priority; no target chain or date has been set. Build chain identifiers, RPC URLs, and contract addresses as configuration, not constants.
47	* On Oct 7, 2025, ICE announced a $2B strategic investment; per the official ICE press release, this was at "a valuation of approximately $8 billion pre-investment," and Polymarket CEO Shayne Coplan posted on X the same day that the investment was "valuing us at $9 billion post-money."
48	* The Polymarket international platform reopened to US users in February 2026; the 2022 CFTC geo-block was lifted. Separate Polymarket US (operated by QCX LLC) runs as the CFTC-regulated entity.
49	* HeartBeats API and post-only orders launched January 2026 for market-maker resilience.
50	
51	#### 1.2 The three APIs
52	
53	| API | Base URL | Purpose | Auth |
54	|---|---|---|---|
55	| **CLOB API** | `https://clob.polymarket.com` | Order book + trading + order management | Public reads; L1 (EIP-712) to mint API creds; L2 (HMAC) for trading |
56	| **Gamma API** | `https://gamma-api.polymarket.com` | Market/event metadata, tags, slugs, sports, search | Public |
57	| **Data API** | `https://data-api.polymarket.com` | Positions, trades, activity, holders, value, leaderboard | Public |
58	| **CLOB WebSocket** | `wss://ws-subscriptions-clob.polymarket.com/ws/{market\|user}` | Real-time market and user channels | Public market; L2 user |
59	
60	#### 1.3 CLOB authentication
61	
62	* **L1 (private key, EIP-712).** Sign the `ClobAuth` struct (`domain.name="ClobAuthDomain"`, `version="1"`, `chainId=137`, message `"This message attests that I control the given wallet"`). Needed only to mint or derive L2 credentials.
63	* **L2 (apiKey + secret + passphrase, HMAC-SHA256).** Generate once via `client.create_or_derive_api_key()`; every trading request carries `POLY_ADDRESS`, `POLY_SIGNATURE`, `POLY_TIMESTAMP`, `POLY_API_KEY`, `POLY_PASSPHRASE` headers.
64	
65	#### 1.4 Order types and microstructure (CLOB V2)
66	
67	* **GTC** (good-till-cancel, resting limit), **GTD** (good-till-date), **FOK** (fill-or-kill — full immediate fill or cancel), **FAK** (fill-and-kill — partial immediate fill, remainder cancelled; replaced what older docs called IOC).
68	* **Tick sizes** per market: one of `0.1, 0.01, 0.001, 0.0001`. Default $0.01; tightens to $0.001 when an outcome price moves above $0.96 or below $0.04. The bot must read `tick_size` per market and subscribe to the `tick_size_change` WS event — non-conforming orders are rejected.
69	* **Minimum order size:** per-market field `min_order_size` (typical `"5"` for limit orders); market orders require ≥ $1 USDC notional.
70	* **Maker fee schedule** is currently 0 bps; market orders pay taker fees per market-specific schedules.
71	
72	#### 1.5 Rate limits (Cloudflare-throttled, queued not rejected)
73	
74	| Endpoint family | Burst (10s) | Sustained (10 min) |
75	|---|---|---|
76	| General REST | 15,000 | — |
77	| CLOB general | 9,000 | — |
78	| CLOB market data (`/book`, `/price`, `/midprice`) single | 1,500 | — |
79	| CLOB market data batch | 500 | — |
80	| POST `/order` | 3,500 | 36,000 (~60/s) |
81	| DELETE `/order` | 3,000 | 30,000 |
82	| POST `/orders` (batch ≤15) | 1,000 | 15,000 |
83	| Gamma API general | 4,000 | — |
84	| Gamma `/events` | 500 | — |
85	| Gamma `/markets` | 300 | — |
86	| Gamma `/search` | 350 | — |
87	| Data API general | 1,000 | — |
88	| Data API `/trades` | 200 | — |
89	| Data API `/positions` | 150 | — |
90	
91	A 50-leader copy bot is multiple orders of magnitude under every limit.
92	
93	#### 1.6 WebSocket channels
94	
95	* **`wss://ws-subscriptions-clob.polymarket.com/ws/market`** — public; subscribe by `assets_ids` (token IDs). Emits `book`, `price_change`, `tick_size_change`, `last_trade_price`. With `custom_feature_enabled: true`: also `best_bid_ask`, `new_market`, `market_resolved`. Server sends ping every 5s; client must reply pong within 10s. Soft cap of approximately 5 concurrent WS connections per IP.
96	* **`wss://ws-subscriptions-clob.polymarket.com/ws/user`** — L2-authenticated; subscribe by `markets` (condition IDs). Emits `trade` and `order` events for **your own** account only. It does NOT report other users' trades — a critical architectural constraint: leader detection must use the market channel or Polygon logs, not the user channel.
97	
98	#### 1.7 Data API — endpoints used by the bot
99	
100	All public, no auth. Base `https://data-api.polymarket.com`. Accepts both EOA and proxy addresses in `user`; responses always return `proxyWallet`.
101	
102	* **`GET /positions?user=<addr>`** — paginated; `limit` default 100, max 500; `sortBy ∈ {TOKENS, CURRENT, INITIAL, CASHPNL, PERCENTPNL, TITLE, RESOLVING, PRICE}`; filters `market` (conditionId CSV), `sizeThreshold`, `redeemable`, `mergeable`, `title`.
103	* **`GET /trades?user=<addr>`** — paginated DESC; filters `market`, `side`, `takerOnly` (default true), `filterType` (CASH|TOKENS), `filterAmount`.
104	* **`GET /activity?user=<addr>`** — TRADE / SPLIT / MERGE / REDEEM / REWARD / CONVERSION events with `start`/`end` unix-seconds windows.
105	* **`GET /holders?market=<conditionId>`** — top holders.
106	* **`GET /value?user=<addr>`** — total USD value of open positions.
107	* **`GET /v1/leaderboard`** — official leaderboard. Params: `category` (OVERALL, POLITICS, SPORTS, CRYPTO, CULTURE, MENTIONS, WEATHER, ECONOMICS, TECH, FINANCE), `timePeriod` (DAY, WEEK, MONTH, ALL), `orderBy` (PNL, VOL), `limit` (default 25, max 50), `offset` (max 1000). Returns `[{rank, proxyWallet, userName, vol, pnl, profileImage, xUsername, verifiedBadge}]`. This is the seed for daily wallet discovery.

109	#### 1.8 Goldsky subgraphs (public, no auth, GraphQL POST)
110	
111	Base: `https://api.goldsky.com/api/public/project_cl6mb8i9h0003e201j6li0diw/subgraphs/<name>/<version>/gn`.
112	
113	| Subgraph | Path | Use in bot |
114	|---|---|---|
115	| Positions | `positions-subgraph/0.0.7/gn` | Cross-check Data API, time series |
116	| Orderbook / Orders | `orderbook-subgraph/0.0.1/gn` | Historical fills, last-trade-price |
117	| Activity | `activity-subgraph/0.0.4/gn` | Splits/merges/redemptions audit |
118	| Open Interest | `oi-subgraph/0.0.6/gn` | Liquidity gating |
119	| PnL | `pnl-subgraph/0.0.14/gn` | Authoritative realized PnL for ranking |
120	
121	The Graph also hosts a combined subgraph at `gateway.thegraph.com/api/<key>/subgraphs/id/Bx1W4S7kDVxs9gC3s2G6DS8kdNBJNVhMviCtin2DiBp` (100k queries/month free with a Graph API key) as a backup if Goldsky has an outage.
122	
123	#### 1.9 The proxy-wallet / funder model — critical
124	
125	Polymarket positions and pUSD balances never live on the EOA. Every Polymarket user has a proxy wallet:
126	
127	* **Magic-link (email) users** → CREATE2-derived **EIP-1167 minimal proxy** (signature type **1**).
128	* **Browser / MetaMask / external-EOA users** → **modified Gnosis Safe (1-of-1 multisig)** (signature type **2**).
129	* **Pure EOA-signed trading** (deposit wallets, API-only setups) → signature type **0**.
130	
131	All ERC-1155 outcome tokens and pUSD balances live on the **proxy address** (the `funder`), not the EOA. APIs report trades against `proxyWallet`. Proxy factory address: `0x56C79347e95530c01A2FC76E732f9566dA16E113`.
132	
133	**Copy-trading implications:**
134	1. The "leader wallet" you track is the **proxy address** (the 0x address on the leaderboard, profile URL, and `proxyWallet` field). Do not follow their EOA — it will appear inactive.
135	2. The bot's own trades emit under *its* proxy address; the `funder` parameter you pass to `py-clob-client-v2` is your proxy.
136	3. To recover the canonical proxy from an EOA, use the deterministic CREATE2 derivation; `py-clob-client-v2` and `rs-clob-client` ship helpers (`derive_safe_wallet`, `derive_proxy_wallet`).
137	
138	#### 1.10 Wallet setup for the bot (decisive)
139	
140	**Use a fresh dedicated EOA + Gnosis Safe proxy (signature type 2).**
141	
142	1. Generate a new EOA locally (`eth_account.Account.create()`); store the private key only in the OS keychain (see §10), not in plain `.env`.
143	2. Send ~$3 of POL to the EOA for gas insurance (allowance-setting, manual wrapping, withdrawals).
144	3. Deploy the Safe proxy by the first interaction with Polymarket, or proactively via the Safe factory.
145	4. Bridge / deposit USDC on Polygon to the **Safe proxy address** (NOT the EOA). First trade auto-wraps USDC → pUSD via the Collateral Onramp.
146	5. Run a one-shot `set_allowances.py` (adapted from NautilusTrader's adapter or the official `agent-skills` repo) to grant max allowance to Exchange V2 and CTF on pUSD and the 1155 tokens.
147	6. Call `client.create_or_derive_api_key()` once; store `{apiKey, secret, passphrase}` in the OS keychain.
148	
149	Per-trade gas cost is essentially zero in USDC terms because Polymarket's Builder Relayer gas-stations CLOB matches; POL is held only as insurance for direct contract calls.
150	
151	#### 1.11 Geographic restrictions — honest note
152	
153	The international platform is open to US users again as of February 2026; the 2022 CFTC block was lifted. It remains blocked in OFAC-sanctioned jurisdictions (Iran, North Korea, Cuba, Russia, Syria) and a growing list of others including France, Belgium, Singapore, Portugal, Hungary, Argentina, and the UK (gambling-licensing actions). A separate **Polymarket US** entity runs as the CFTC-regulated platform. The bot does not bypass geofencing; verify your specific jurisdiction and contract type before going live, and respect ToS.

156	
157	### 2. Wallet discovery & ranking
158	
159	#### 2.1 Objective
160	
161	Maintain a daily-refreshed roster of 20–50 "fast" wallets — high trade frequency, short hold times, small/medium per-trade sizes — re-computed every day at 04:00 UTC and persisted with full diff history.
162	
163	#### 2.2 Sources, ranked
164	
165	1. **Data API `/v1/leaderboard`** — primary seed. Pull DAY, WEEK, MONTH × OVERALL/POLITICS/SPORTS/CRYPTO. With `limit=50, offset=0,50,…,500`, ~500 wallets/category/window. ~30 requests/day.
166	2. **Goldsky `pnl-subgraph`** — authoritative 30-day realized PnL ranking.
167	3. **Goldsky `orderbook-subgraph` + `activity-subgraph`** — trade counts, hold times, market diversity.
168	4. **Data API `/trades` + `/positions` per candidate** — drill-down.
169	5. **Data API `/value`** — current open-positions $ for the size band filter.
170	6. **Public Dune dashboards (free tier, 2,500 credits/month; pay-as-you-go at $5.00 per 100 additional credits beyond the bucket; ~500 query executions/month free)** — cross-validation:
171	   * `dune.com/rchen8/polymarket`
172	   * `dune.com/filarm/polymarket-activity`
173	   * `dune.com/datadashboards/polymarket-overview`
174	   * `dune.com/defioasis/polymarket-pnl`
175	   * `dune.com/genejp999/polymarket-leaderboard`
176	   * `dune.com/lujanodera/polymarket-analysis`
177	7. **Third-party analytics (cross-check only):** `polymarketanalytics.com/traders`, `polymarketrankings.com`, `polycopy.app`, `stand.trade`, `polytrackhq.app`, `merlin.trade/leaderboard`. All scrape the Polymarket APIs.
178	8. **Alchemy Polygon free RPC** — direct chain queries for ground-truth and cluster detection.
179	9. **Ankr + public `polygon-rpc.com`** — failover RPCs.
180	
181	#### 2.3 Discovery funnel (daily)
182	
183	```
184	Step 1 — Seed (Data API + Goldsky pnl-subgraph):
185	    Top 500 wallets by 7-day PNL (Data API leaderboard)
186	    Top 500 wallets by 30-day PNL (Goldsky pnl-subgraph)
187	    Top 200 by VOL on each of DAY/WEEK/MONTH (Data API)
188	    Union ≈ 1,200-1,500 candidate proxy addresses.
189	
190	Step 2 — Hard filters (drop immediately):
191	    - 30d trade count < 30           → statistical noise
192	    - 30d trade count > 5,000        → likely MM bot, not "fast directional"
193	    - Current /value > $250,000      → too large; your $5 copies are noise; their fills move price
194	    - Current /value < $1,000        → too small / dust-farmer
195	    - First-trade timestamp < 21d ago → insufficient history
196	    - Wash-trading score > 0.3       → drop (see §2.5)
197	    - Cluster sibling already in roster → drop dupes (see §2.5)
198	
199	Step 3 — Score remaining ≈ 200 candidates (see §2.4).
200	
201	Step 4 — Top 50 by score: top 30 "active", 31-50 "standby".
202	```
203	
204	#### 2.4 Ranking score — exact formula
205	
206	For each wallet over a 30-day rolling window:
207	
208	```
209	trades_30d        = taker trades from /trades
210	trade_freq        = trades_30d / 30
211	median_hold_hours = median(close_ts - open_ts) per closed position
212	win_rate          = closed_winners / closed_trades
213	realized_pnl_30d  = sum realized PnL on closed trades (pnl-subgraph)
214	avg_position_usd  = mean(initialValue) of opens in window
215	per_trade_pnl     = realized_pnl_30d / closed_trades_30d
216	per_trade_pnl_std = stddev of per-trade realized PnL
217	sharpe_like       = per_trade_pnl / per_trade_pnl_std       (clip to [-3, 3])
218	market_diversity  = unique conditionIds / trades_30d         (clip to [0, 1])
219	recent_7d_pnl     = realized PnL over last 7 days
220	```
221	
222	Percentile-normalize each within the candidate pool, then:
223	
224	```
225	score = 0.25 * pct(trade_freq)              # FAST = trade often
226	      + 0.20 * pct(sharpe_like)             # consistent per-trade edge
227	      + 0.15 * pct(win_rate)
228	      + 0.15 * pct(recent_7d_pnl)           # recent form
229	      + 0.10 * (1 - pct(median_hold_hours)) # SHORT holds preferred
230	      + 0.10 * pct(market_diversity)        # not a one-market specialist
231	      + 0.05 * pct(realized_pnl_30d)        # de-emphasized at small bankroll
232	```
233	
234	Tie-breaks: higher `trades_30d`, then later `last_trade_ts`. Top 30 = active, 31–50 = standby.
235	
236	#### 2.5 Survivorship-bias and anti-gaming guardrails
237	
238	* Minimum 30 closed trades, minimum 21-day account age, positive realized 30d PnL, max 35% 30d drawdown, multiplicative 0.5× penalty when `last_trade_ts > 48h`.
239	* **Wash-trading score (drop if > 0.3)** is a weighted sum of:
240	  1. Counterparty concentration (HHI > 0.4 in any 24h window over `/activity` + `orderFilledEvent`s) → +0.5.
241	  2. Round-trip clustering: buy-then-sell of `(conditionId, outcomeIndex)` within ≤10 min netting near-zero share-balance change; > 15% of trades → +0.3.
242	  3. Extreme-price trades (price < $0.01 or > $0.99) > 20% of trades → +0.4.
243	  4. Volume/PnL imbalance: `|realized_pnl_30d| / total_volume_30d < 0.001` at volume > $50k → +0.4.
244	* **Cluster detection by funding-source analysis.** For each candidate's controlling EOA (resolved by reading the Safe singleton's owner via Polygon RPC), trace USDC funding hops via `eth_getLogs`. Edge: "EOA A funded EOA B with ≥ $100 USDC within 7 days of B's first trade." In any weakly-connected component, keep only the highest-scoring wallet.
245	
246	These guardrails are not theoretical: the Columbia / Barnard paper *"Network-Based Detection of Wash Trading"* (SSRN, Nov. 6, 2025) by Yash Kanoria and Hongyao Ma (Columbia Business School), Rajiv Sethi (Barnard College), and doctoral student Allen Sirolly states that "the 'artificial trading'…accounted for an average of 25% of all buying and selling on Polymarket over the past three years." Treat naive leaderboard scraping as poisoned.
247	
248	#### 2.6 Cold-wallet rule (intra-day)
249	
250	Pause copying from a wallet (without removing from the roster):
251	* Last detected trade > 24 h ago.
252	* 7-day rolling realized PnL turns negative AND drawdown > 15% from 30d peak.
253	* Three consecutive copied trades from this leader closed at a loss in last 48 h.
254	
255	Resume automatically when conditions reverse.
256	
257	---
258	
259	### 3. Copy execution engine
260	
261	#### 3.1 Detection — hybrid
262	
263	Because the CLOB user WebSocket cannot subscribe to *other users'* wallets, the bot uses:
264	
265	* **(a) Polygon log subscription via Alchemy `eth_subscribe("logs", …)`** on the V2 Exchange contract's `OrderFilled` / `OrdersMatched` events, filtered for active leader proxy addresses in `maker`/`taker`. Polygon block propagation is ~2s; the event reaches the bot within ~50–200 ms of inclusion. Alchemy's free tier covers a continuous Polygon log subscription comfortably.
266	* **(b) Polling `data-api.polymarket.com/trades?user=<wallet>&limit=20` per leader on a 3 s stagger.** Data API picks up matches ~500 ms after off-chain match — *before* chain settlement. **Primary signal path.** Thirty leaders × every 3 s = 10 req/s, well under the 200/10s `/trades` limit.
267	* **(c) Goldsky `orderbook-subgraph` polling at 10 s** as a reconciliation/audit layer, ~5–30 s lagged.
268	
269	**Latency budget (leader fill → bot order submitted):**
270	
271	| Stage | Typical | Worst case |
272	|---|---|---|
273	| Leader fill matched off-chain | t = 0 | t = 0 |
274	| Data API `/trades` updated | +300 ms | +900 ms |
275	| Bot poll picks up new trade | +1,500 ms (3 s poll avg) | +3,000 ms |
276	| Decision logic (dedup, sizing, risk) | +20 ms | +100 ms |
277	| Order signing (EIP-712) | +30 ms | +80 ms |
278	| POST `/order` to CLOB | +150 ms | +600 ms |
279	| **End-to-end** | **~2 s** | **~4.7 s** |
280	
281	When the log-subscription path (a) fires first (often once the chain settlement is included), end-to-end can be ~600–800 ms. **Two seconds is the realistic target.** Sub-second is the aspiration.
282	
283	#### 3.2 Sizing — decisive
284	
285	**Fixed-dollar with leader-rank multiplier**, NOT proportional-to-leader-bankroll. A leader's bankroll cannot be reliably estimated, and copying their *percentage* on $500 produces $0.50 trades that violate the per-market `min_order_size` of 5 shares.
286	
287	```
288	PER_TRADE_BASE_USD   = 5.00
289	SIZE_RANK_MULTIPLIER = {
290	   ranks 1-10  : 1.50  ($7.50)
291	   ranks 11-20 : 1.00  ($5.00)
292	   ranks 21-30 : 0.70  ($3.50, floor enforced below)
293	}
294	MIN_NOTIONAL_USD     = 5.00
295	MAX_NOTIONAL_USD     = 10.00
296	SLIPPAGE_BUFFER      = 0.02  # cap effective impact at 2% of leader's fill
297	```
298	
299	Per copy: `size_usd = clip(PER_TRADE_BASE_USD * SIZE_RANK_MULTIPLIER[rank], MIN, MAX)`. Shares = `floor(size_usd / p_leader)`; reject if `shares < market.min_order_size`. Clamp price source to `min(p_leader, top_of_book_ask)` to handle the p ≥ $1 edge case.
300	
301	**Why this works on $500:** Max 25 concurrent positions (§3.7) × $5–$7 average = $125–$175 allocated, leaving 65 %+ free for new signals. Average copy is 5–10 shares, comfortably above the 5-share floor.
302	
303	#### 3.3 Aggregation: multiple leaders, same market
304	
305	When ≥ 2 leaders enter the **same (conditionId, outcomeIndex)** within a **120 s rolling window**:
306	
307	* The first signal opens the position at chosen size.
308	* Each subsequent signal in the window from a *different* leader adds 50 % of base sizing, capped at `MAX_NOTIONAL_USD × 3 = $30` total exposure per market.
309	* The position is tagged with all contributing leaders; PnL attribution is split proportionally.
310	
311	**Do not** open separate orders per leader for the same market — that triples slippage. Single `clientOrderId` per aggregated entry.
312	
313	#### 3.4 Routing — decisive
314	
315	**FOK market-style orders priced at `min(leader_fill_price + 2 ticks, top_of_book_ask)`.** Reasoning:
316	
317	* GTC limits at the leader's exact fill price are appealing (zero maker fee) but in fast markets frequently fail to fill before the edge decays — adverse selection is the dominant copy-trading risk.
318	* FOK guarantees fill at predictable slippage or no trade.
319	* Polymarket's maker fee schedule is currently 0 bps, so the financial incentive to be a maker is small.
320	* Sells (exits): same FOK logic at `max(leader_exit_price - 2 ticks, top_of_book_bid)`.
321	
322	If FOK fails (book moved past +2 ticks during submission), do not retry — log and move on; the signal has decayed.
323	
324	#### 3.5 Exit logic
325	
326	Each position carries `{leader_proxies, market_id, outcome, entry_ts, entry_size, entry_price}`. Continuous triggers:
327	
328	1. **Leader-exit mirror.** Any contributing leader scales out ≥ 50 % of their position → bot sells the same fractional %. When aggregated leader exposure hits zero → fully close.
329	2. **Time stop.** `now - entry_ts > 24 h` AND no leader has updated their position in 12+ h → close at market.
330	3. **Market-resolution stop.** When `time_to_resolution < 6 h`, freeze sizing (no new opens), exit any existing position with unrealized PnL > +5 %. Resolution risk is unmodelable.
331	4. **Hard PnL stops.** Per-position stop-loss at −40 % of notional; daily aggregate loss > 8 % of bankroll triggers kill-switch.
332	
333	#### 3.6 Conflict resolution: YES vs NO
334	
335	* No current position: take the **first** signal.
336	* Already hold YES, new NO signal: ignore NO. The thesis is owned by the held position.
337	* Both signals in the same 120 s window: take the **higher-scoring leader's** side. Log rejected signal with full reasoning.
338	
339	#### 3.7 Capacity limits ($500 bankroll)
340	
341	| Limit | Value | Rationale |
342	|---|---|---|
343	| Max concurrent open positions | 25 | Diversification floor, room for new signals |
344	| Max exposure per single market | $30 (≈ 6 % bankroll) | One resolution failure capped at 6 % |
345	| Max exposure per single leader (sole contributor) | $50 (10 %) | Concentration cap |
346	| Max daily new openings | 40 trades | Spread/fee budget cap |
347	| Daily loss kill-switch | −8 % ($40 from session start) | Stop the bleed, force review |
348	| Per-position stop-loss | −40 % of notional | ~$2 max single-position loss |
349	
350	#### 3.8 Idempotency and crash recovery
351	
352	Every order carries a deterministic `clientOrderId = sha256(leader_proxy + market_id + outcome + leader_fill_tx_hash + bot_size_bucket)[:16]`. SQLite `attempted_orders` table is written **before** POST. On recovery:
353	
354	1. Load rows with `status IN ('pending', 'submitted')`.
355	2. Query `client.get_order(server_order_id)` or `/trades?user=<my_proxy>&limit=…` filtered by token+price.
356	3. Reconcile: matched on-chain → FILLED, populate positions; still open → keep tracking; timed out > 5 min → CANCELED.
357	4. Re-arm signal processing only after reconciliation completes.
358	
359	A `kill -9` mid-trade cannot result in a double order.
360	
361	---
362	
363	### 4. Paper trading mode
364	
365	#### 4.1 Single code path
366	
367	```python
368	def execute_order(intent: OrderIntent, mode: Mode):
369	    # ... all upstream logic identical ...
370	    if mode is Mode.LIVE:
371	        return clob_client.create_and_post_order(...)
372	    return paper_fill_engine.simulate(intent, current_book)
373	```
374	
375	The mode is a single config flag; the dashboard shows it as a giant header badge (red PAPER / green LIVE).
376	
377	#### 4.2 Realistic simulator
378	
379	The simulator subscribes to the same `wss://…/ws/market` channel and maintains an in-memory L2 book per active market. On `simulate(intent)`:
380	
381	1. Snapshot the book at intent time.
382	2. For a market BUY FOK with notional `N`, walk asks from best ask upward, consuming size until `N` is filled or book exhausts.
383	3. **Pessimism:** apply +0.5-cent worst-side adjustment to the VWAP fill price (your real order would move the book by up to one tick), AND drop 25 % of visible size at each level (other takers + latency exist). Paper fills are slightly *worse* than reality — desirable conservatism.
384	4. Simulated fees: 0 bps current; engine retains a `paper_fee_rate_bps` config for what-if testing.
385	5. Write a `paper_fill` row with `(client_order_id, fill_price, fill_size, fees_usd, slippage_bps, book_snapshot_id)`.
386	
387	FOK that the simulator decides would fail (insufficient depth within +2 ticks) returns `status=REJECTED` and opens no position.
388	
389	#### 4.3 PnL, fees, slippage tracking
390	
391	Three accumulating tables: `paper_positions` / `live_positions` (parallel schemas), `pnl_events(ts, mode, position_id, realized_delta, unrealized_delta, reason)`, and `cost_breakdown(ts, mode, position_id, gross_pnl, fees_usd, slippage_usd, net_pnl)` where `slippage_usd = (fill_price - mid_price_at_intent) * size`.
392	
393	The dashboard shows both gross and net-of-fees+slippage PnL.
394	
395	#### 4.4 Data structure — decisive
396	
397	**Two parallel ledgers** (`paper_*` and `live_*` tables with identical schemas), NOT a single ledger with a `mode` tag. Forces zero cross-contamination, simplifies SQL, makes paper↔live a clean cutover, and allows running paper indefinitely alongside live for ongoing validation.
398	
399	```
400	paper_trades / live_trades        # every order intent, attempt, outcome
401	paper_positions / live_positions  # open and historical positions
402	paper_fills / live_fills          # granular per-fill records
403	events                            # single shared log with mode tag
404	leader_signals                    # mode-independent
405	leader_rankings                   # daily roster snapshot
406	```
407	
408	---
409	
410	### 5. Logging & observability
411	
412	#### 5.1 Stack — chosen
413	
414	`structlog` 24.x + stdlib `logging` JSONFormatter, writing to (a) stdout, (b) a daily-rotating file, (c) a SQLite `events` table the dashboard reads directly. No Loki/Grafana — overkill for a single-user local bot.
415	
416	#### 5.2 Logged events (categories)
417	
418	Every event: `ts, trace_id, span_id, level, event_name, mode, source, payload, parent_trace_id?`.
419	
420	* **Leader detection:** `leader_trade_detected`.
421	* **Ranking:** `leader_roster_built`, `leader_score_changed`, `wallet_added`, `wallet_dropped`, `wallet_clustered_with`.
422	* **Decision:** `signal_received`, `signal_dedup_skipped`, `signal_aggregated`, `signal_rejected` (with reason), `sizing_applied`.
423	* **Order lifecycle:** `order_built`, `order_signed`, `order_submitted`, `order_accepted`, `order_filled`, `order_partial_fill`, `order_canceled`, `order_rejected`.
424	* **Positions:** `position_opened`, `position_increased`, `position_decreased`, `position_closed`.
425	* **PnL:** `pnl_realized`, `pnl_unrealized_snapshot` (every 30 s).
426	* **System:** `websocket_connected`, `websocket_disconnected` (with code/reason), `websocket_reconnect_attempt`, `rate_limit_hit`, `rpc_error`, `subgraph_lag_detected`, `bot_started`, `bot_stopped`, `mode_switched`.
427	* **Errors:** `exception_caught` (full traceback), `recovery_started`, `recovery_completed`, `kill_switch_triggered`.
428	
429	#### 5.3 Trace IDs
430	
431	When `leader_trade_detected` fires, a UUIDv7 (time-sortable) `trace_id` is created. All downstream events inherit it via `structlog.contextvars`. By the time `order_filled` fires, every intermediate decision is queryable as `SELECT * FROM events WHERE trace_id = ?`.
432	
433	#### 5.4 Storage
434	
435	* **SQLite (WAL mode)** is the operational store. Settings: `journal_mode=WAL`, `synchronous=NORMAL`, `cache_size=-65536`, `mmap_size=268435456`. Daily VACUUM after the 04:00 UTC ranking job.
436	* **DuckDB** (with the `sqlite_scanner` extension) for analytical queries on the events table — no copy needed. The dashboard's log explorer and analytics views use DuckDB.
437	
438	#### 5.5 Metrics
439	
440	**Direct SQLite queries from the dashboard.** FastAPI's `GET /metrics` returns canned aggregations as JSON. Dashboard polls every 5 s for top-line; live events fan out via WebSocket.
441

444	### 6. Dashboard — the cool one
445	
446	#### 6.1 Stack — decisive
447	
448	**Next.js 15 (App Router) + React + TypeScript + Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui + TradingView Lightweight Charts v5 + TanStack Query + a single FastAPI WebSocket for live push.**
449	
450	Not Streamlit, not Dash, not Tauri, not SvelteKit. Reasoning:
451	* Next.js + shadcn is the modern dark-trader-terminal aesthetic.
452	* The `coinpulse` reference (Next.js 16 + shadcn + TradingView Lightweight Charts) is precisely this pattern.
453	* TradingView Lightweight Charts v5 (current version 5.2.0, Apache-2.0 license, ~190 KB bundle size) avoids the licensing complexity of TradingView's proprietary Charting Library. Per Socket.dev's npm package analysis: "The npm package lightweight-charts receives a total of 263,815 weekly downloads," confirming ecosystem support.
454	* Tauri/Electron add packaging complexity for no benefit when the dashboard always runs locally.
455	
456	#### 6.2 Topology
457	
458	```
459	   ┌───────────────┐    ws + REST     ┌────────────────────┐
460	   │   Browser     │ ←──────────────→ │ FastAPI :8787      │
461	   │ Next.js :3000 │                  │ control plane      │
462	   └───────────────┘                  └────────────────────┘
463	                                              │
464	                                              ▼
465	                                       ┌────────────────┐
466	                                       │ SQLite (WAL)   │
467	                                       │ bot.db         │
468	                                       └────────────────┘
469	                                              ▲
470	                                              │
471	                                       ┌────────────────┐
472	                                       │ Bot process    │
473	                                       │ (asyncio)      │
474	                                       └────────────────┘
475	```
476	
477	Both processes read/write the SQLite file (WAL handles concurrency). The bot publishes events into a `events_outbox` table polled at 100 ms by FastAPI, which fans events out to the browser via a single `/ws` connection.
478	
479	#### 6.3 Dashboard views
480	
481	* **A. Overview** — mode badge, equity curve (Lightweight Charts line series), today's gross/net PnL, trade count, win rate, hit rate, max drawdown today, bankroll free vs allocated, kill-switch progress.
482	* **B. Positions** — live table `market | outcome | leader(s) | entry | mark | size | unrealized $ | unrealized % | age | exit state`. Row click → market chart with overlay of bot entry/scale-ins/exits and contributing leaders' entries/exits on the same time axis.
483	* **C. Closed positions ledger** — filterable, with realized PnL, fees, slippage breakdown, per-leader attribution.
484	* **D. Leader leaderboard** — current 30 active + 20 standby, daily/weekly stats, score, score delta vs yesterday, contribution to bot PnL, enable/disable toggle per leader, cold indicator.
485	* **E. Markets** — markets the bot is in, time-to-resolution, depth chart (shadcn-styled).
486	* **F. Live log feed** — streaming via WS, filterable by category/leader/market/trace ID/level. Click trace → vertical timeline from `leader_trade_detected` → `order_filled`.
487	* **G. Daily re-ranking results** — diff vs yesterday: wallets added/dropped, rank ≥ 5 changes, score ≥ 0.1 changes; sparklines of each wallet's 30 d PnL.
488	* **H. System health** — WS status (CLOB market, log subscription, FastAPI↔browser), rolling-window rate-limit usage, last leader trade detected per leader, error counts by category, process uptime.
489	* **I. Settings** — pause/resume, kill switch, per-leader toggles, mode toggle (paper↔live) gated behind a "type ENABLE LIVE" modal AND the §9 validation precondition.
490	* **J. Analytics** — equity curve, 7-d rolling win rate, trade frequency, hold-time distribution histogram, slippage analysis (paper vs live deltas), per-leader PnL contribution waterfall.
491	
492	#### 6.4 Aesthetic
493	
494	Dark theme by default. JetBrains Mono for tickers. shadcn/ui throughout; no custom CSS for primitives. Bloomberg-style information density. Live counters animate via Framer Motion.

498	### 7. Tech stack — final, decisive
499	
500	| Layer | Choice | Why |
501	|---|---|---|
502	| Bot language | Python 3.11 | `py-clob-client-v2` is official |
503	| Async runtime | `asyncio` + `httpx` (HTTP) + `websockets` (raw WS) + `gql` (GraphQL) | Standard, well-supported |
504	| Polymarket SDK | **`py-clob-client-v2`** (V1 deprecated April 2026) | Official |
505	| EVM access | `web3.py` 7.x | Polygon RPC + log subscription |
506	| Process supervisor | `honcho` (Procfile) for dev; `supervisord` or systemd user unit for prod | Lightweight; no Docker needed |
507	| Database | SQLite 3.45+ (WAL) | Zero-ops, ACID, perfect for single-user |
508	| Analytical queries | DuckDB 1.x with `sqlite_scanner` | Fast aggregations over SQLite |
509	| Logging | `structlog` 24.x + stdlib `logging` JSONFormatter | Structured, trace IDs |
510	| Control plane | FastAPI + uvicorn (`127.0.0.1:8787`) | REST + WS, trivial DX |
511	| Dashboard framework | Next.js 15 (App Router) + TypeScript | De-facto modern stack |
512	| UI components | shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4 + Lucide icons | Free "cool" aesthetic |
513	| Charts | **TradingView Lightweight Charts v5.2.0** (Apache-2.0) | Smallest/fastest financial charts; no license complexity |
514	| Data fetching | TanStack Query v5 + custom WS hook | Cache + invalidation discipline |
515	| Animations | Framer Motion | Smooth number tickers |
516	| Type validation | Pydantic v2 | Runtime safety on all API responses |
517	| Secrets | OS keychain via `keyring` Python | macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager / Linux Secret Service |
518	| Dependency mgmt | `uv` (Python); `pnpm` (JS) | Fastest installs in 2026 |
519	
520	#### 7.1 Bot ↔ dashboard wire protocol
521	
522	* REST: `GET /api/state`, `GET /api/positions`, `GET /api/leaders`, `POST /api/settings/{key}`, `POST /api/kill-switch`, `POST /api/mode` (gated).
523	* WebSocket: single `/ws` pushing JSON typed by `kind`: `event`, `position_update`, `pnl_tick`, `leader_signal`, `system_health`.
524	
525	#### 7.2 Deployment (local)
526	
527	```bash
528	# install
529	git clone <repo> polymarket-bot && cd polymarket-bot
530	uv venv && uv pip install -e ".[dev]"        # backend
531	cd dashboard && pnpm install                  # frontend
532	cp .env.example .env                          # fill keys; see §10
533	python -m bot.scripts.bootstrap               # mint CLOB API keys, set allowances, smoke test
534	# run
535	honcho start                                  # bot + fastapi
536	cd dashboard && pnpm dev                      # → http://localhost:3000
537	```
538	
539	For always-on machines, wrap with `supervisord` or a systemd user unit (documented in `OPS.md`).

667	### 9. Phased build plan
668	
669	#### Phase 1 — Data layer + wallet discovery & ranking (read-only)
670	
671	**Goal:** Daily discovery job producing a defensible roster, no ability to place orders.
672	
673	* Repo scaffolded, `uv` env, pre-commit, `ruff` + `pytest` CI.
674	* Pydantic settings + populated `.env.example`.
675	* SQLite migrations: `leader_candidates`, `leader_rosters`, `events`, `cluster_graph`.
676	* Clients: Gamma, Data API, 5 Goldsky subgraphs, Polygon RPC (Alchemy primary, Ankr + public fallback).
677	* Daily job: leaderboard pull, candidate union, hard filters, scoring, cluster detection, roster persistence.
678	* CLI `dump_roster.py` prints today's roster with reasoning.
679	* **Done when:** `python -m bot.discovery.job` runs cleanly twice in a row, produces 30–50 wallets with audit trail; integration test replays a fixed leader through the wash-trading detector and produces the expected score.
680	
681	#### Phase 2 — Paper trading engine
682	
683	**Goal:** End-to-end paper copies with realistic fill simulation.
684	
685	* WS market-channel client with reconnect/heartbeat; in-memory L2 book per market.
686	* Polygon log subscription on Exchange V2 with leader-proxy filter.
687	* Data API `/trades` polling per leader (3 s stagger).
688	* Signal dedup, aggregation, sizing, routing.
689	* Paper fill engine with pessimism adjustment.
690	* Exit logic, conflict resolution, capacity limits.
691	* Crash-recovery reconciliation tested with `kill -9` mid-trade.
692	* Structured logging with trace IDs end-to-end.
693	* **Done when:** Bot runs 7 consecutive days paper, ≥ 200 simulated trades, every copy has a complete trace chain in `events`, and replaying any trace ID in pure code reproduces the same decision.
694	
695	#### Phase 3 — Dashboard
696	
697	**Goal:** All 10 views (§6.3) wired and live.
698	
699	* Next.js 15 + Tailwind + shadcn scaffold, dark theme.
700	* FastAPI control plane (REST + WS fanout).
701	* Overview (equity curve via Lightweight Charts) + Positions + Closed ledger.
702	* Leader leaderboard + Markets + Live log.
703	* Re-ranking diff page + System health.
704	* Analytics page (histograms, win rate over time).
705	* Settings with gated mode toggle.
706	* **Done when:** Browser stays connected 24 hours without reconnect glitches; every panel shows live data; mode toggle correctly gated.
707	
708	#### Phase 4 — Live trading (gated)
709	
710	**Validation criteria — all must hold within last 14 days to unlock live:**
711	
712	* ≥ 14 consecutive days of paper running.
713	* ≥ 400 paper trades executed.
714	* Net paper PnL after simulated fees + slippage > **+3 %** on bankroll.
715	* Paper Sharpe-like (per-day net return / std-dev over the 14 days) > **0.5**.
716	* Max paper daily drawdown ≤ **8 %**.
717	* Zero "ghost" trades (no trade in ledger without a `leader_trade_detected` parent).
718	* Zero unrecovered crash incidents.
719	
720	Live milestones:
721	1. Run `set_allowances.py` on Polygon.
722	2. Mint L2 credentials via `bootstrap.py`.
723	3. Fund the Safe proxy with $500 USDC on Polygon.
724	4. Enable LIVE in dashboard settings; paper keeps running in parallel.
725	5. **First 72 h: half-size live** (sizing multiplier 0.5×) with manual monitoring of every trade.
726	6. After 72 h with no anomalies: full live sizing.
727	7. **Done when:** 30 days live, live PnL within 1σ of paper PnL over the same period.

730	
731	### 10. Risk & failure modes
732	
733	#### 10.1 Operational risks
734	
735	* **API outages.** Every API call wrapped in `tenacity` retry with exponential backoff + jitter. After 3 retries, pause *new openings* but continue honoring *exits*.
736	* **WebSocket disconnects.** Exponential reconnect (1, 2, 4, 8, …, capped 60 s), re-subscribe, re-fetch book. Every reconnect logged.
737	* **RPC failures.** Multi-provider failover: Alchemy → Ankr → `polygon-rpc.com`. Health-check every 30 s; swap primary if Alchemy 5xx-rate > 10 % in last 5 min.
738	* **Subgraph lag.** Goldsky lags chain tip 5–30 s; subgraph data never used for trading decisions, only ranking and audit. Lag > 5 min surfaces in system health.
739	* **Polymarket chain migration risk.** April 2026 VP-Eng statement confirmed chain migration as a roadmap item. Every chain-specific constant lives behind a `Chain` config; a switch is a config + endpoint change, not a rewrite.
740	
741	#### 10.2 Strategy / market risks (honest)
742	
743	* **Smart-money wallets going cold.** Cold-wallet rule (§2.6) + daily re-ranking + per-leader attribution surface this fast.
744	* **Adverse selection.** You are by definition last to the trade; leader's edge decays in proportion to copy-volume. The +2-tick FOK price cap and aggregation cap mitigate. Expected live slippage relative to paper: ~30–80 bps in fast markets — reflected in the realistic paper pessimism.
745	* **Fees + slippage on a thin bankroll.** $500 × ~5 trades/day ≈ $25/day flow; spread costs of 50–200 bps per round-trip = $0.50–$1.00/day implicit cost. Year-long profitability requires leader net edge to clear ~5–10 % net of costs. Per Andrey Sergeenkov's April 6, 2026 on-chain analysis via Dune Analytics covering 2.5 million addresses on Polygon, 15.9 % of traders are in profit; only 2 % have lifetime profits above $1,000; and only 0.033 % (840 addresses) have earned more than $100,000. Profitability is the exception, not the norm, and the bot must be measured against that base rate.
746	* **Resolution risk.** Markets can resolve against an apparently-correct directional read. The §3.5 "6 hours to resolution" exit forces position close-out before resolution exposure.
747	* **Wash-trading contamination.** The antigaming layer catches obvious patterns; novel ones will slip through. Weekly manual review of top-contributing leaders is in `OPS.md`.
748	
749	#### 10.3 Security risks
750	
751	* **Private key exposure.** Default: key in OS keychain via Python `keyring` (macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager / Linux Secret Service). The `.env` carries only the keychain reference, not the key. File mode 600 on `.env`, gitignored.
752	* **Stretch:** hardware wallet via `web3.py`'s Ledger middleware or a custom signer. Hardware signing adds ~500–800 ms per order — within the 2-second target. Optional but documented.
753	* **Logging redaction.** structlog has a serializer that redacts substrings `key`, `private_key`, `secret`, `passphrase`, `mnemonic`. Never logs the key.
754	* **Local network surface.** FastAPI binds to `127.0.0.1` only; dashboard on `localhost`. Remote access via `ssh -L` port-forwarding, never by exposing FastAPI.
755	* **Dependency supply chain.** `uv` and `pnpm` verify hashes; lockfiles committed; Dependabot enabled.
756	
757	#### 10.4 Compliance & ToS
758	
759	The bot does not bypass any geofencing. The user is responsible for being in a permitted jurisdiction and trading permitted contract types. International Polymarket reopened to US users in Feb 2026, but state-level restrictions apply to specific contract types — confirm before going live.
760
